Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:01 ET
P/C divergence reveals institution dispersion plays into critical NFP print
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts dominate (P/C 1.41) while single-name flow tilts neutral (0.95) — classic institutional hedge structure into NFP tomorrow.
  • 2. Negative gamma at -$2.8B SPY amplifies any NFP surprise, with dealers forced to chase moves above $661 flip.
  • 3. Vol backwardation across 13 names prices fear into this week's data — rich near-term protection before Friday's jobs number.
NARRATIVE — pre-NFP positioning tension

The most striking signal today is the 0.46 divergence between index and single-name P/C ratios, with institutions loading index puts (1.41) while maintaining neutral single-name exposure (0.95). This classic dispersion hedge structure into tomorrow's NFP reflects sophisticated positioning — hedging portfolio beta while maintaining single-name alpha opportunities. The correlation regime at 0.19 supports this trade, offering efficient stock-specific vol relative to index protection. Dealer positioning creates an explosive setup for any NFP surprise, with SPY's -$2.8B negative gamma forcing dealers to amplify moves. The $661 gamma flip sits just $3.40 above current levels, meaning any upside surprise on jobs data triggers mechanical buying acceleration. XLF's massive 22K call block at Aug 53C reflects this positioning, with financials levered to rates repricing on employment strength. Vol surface tensions reveal the week's anxiety, with 13 names in backwardation pricing elevated near-term fear into Friday's print. The divergence flags across energy (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT in XLE, XOM) and financials (XLF) suggest sectors are pricing different NFP outcomes — energy expecting vol expansion, financials positioned for rates volatility rather than directional fear.

Layer 1: Sentiment -9Layer 2: GEX -$2.8BLayer 3: P/C divergence 0.46Layer 9: NFP tomorrow

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
